checks.a_analysis: The output JSON consists entirely of text and data that can be traced back to the source PDF. All headings, paragraphs, and lists correspond to the information provided on page 1 and page 2 of the original document. No invented sections or hallucinated body text were found.

I verified all contact information provided in the final section of the document. The name 'Bob Coats', the email address 'Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov', and the phone number '(919) 807-4781' all match the source exactly.

The output represents both pages of the source PDF. Key sections such as the sidebar information (Representation, Funding, Planning) on page 1, the main body text on both pages, the infographic data, and the specific program descriptions (BAS, LUCA, PSAP, CCC, GSS/SDRP) on page 2 are all present.

The document contains a table representing the timeline graphic from page 2. Since this is an interpretation of a complex graphic into a structured table format, and the source does not contain a formal table, this check is passed. The table successfully distills the major points of the timeline into columns.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: While the timeline graphic on page 2 is represented by a summary table, significant specific information is omitted. The milestones for 2019 ('On-the-ground canvassing where necessary') and 2021 ('April 1: Release Redistricting Summary Files [P.L. 94-171]') are completely missing. Other details like 'award major contracts' (2016), 'on-the-ground interviews begin' (2020), and 'apportionment' (2021) are also lost in the summarization. These omissions exceed the acceptable threshold for a content section.
checks.b_evidence_source: The timeline on Page 2 includes milestones: '2019: On-the-ground canvassing where necessary' and '2021: April 1: Release Redistricting Summary Files [P.L. 94-171]'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The summary table representing this timeline (caption: 'Timeline of Census Preparation Activities') omits these milestones entirely.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 2, Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do!, timeline graphic (converted to table index 0)

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The summary table for the timeline has several temporal misalignments. Activities listed in the source for 2016 ('Develop and award major contracts...') are grouped under the '2017 - 2018' column. Activities for 2018 ('Conduct end-to-end systems test') are grouped under the '2019 - 2021' column. This alters the factual schedule of the government operations described.
checks.b_evidence_source: 2016: Develop and award major contracts...; 2018: Conduct end-to-end systems test.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output table lists 'Develop systems' under the '2017 - 2018' column and 'Conduct tests' under the '2019 - 2021' column.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 2, Preparing for the Census – We Have Work To Do!, timeline graphic (converted to table index 0)

checks.a_analysis: The output was reviewed for repeated text blocks or sections. Each paragraph and heading in the output corresponds to a single instance in the source document. No content duplication was found.

The heading hierarchy is logical and accessible. The main section title is 'North Carolina and the 2020 Census'. Secondary headings like 'Census Day is April 1, 2020!' use H2. Sidebar topics (Representation, Funding, Planning) and program titles (BAS, LUCA, PSAP) are consistently assigned H3 levels. No heading levels are skipped.

The visual timeline at the top of page 2 in the source document has been converted into a 3-column table in the output. This is a correct structural interpretation for accessibility, grouping the chronological events into logical columns (2014-2016, 2017-2018, 2019-2021) that accurately reflect the data points found in the source graphic.

The reading order follows the logical flow of the document. On page 1, it moves from the header to the primary informational text, then integrates the sidebars, and concludes with the bottom infographic. On page 2, it proceeds from the timeline to the detailed program descriptions and finishes with the contact information footer. This matches the intended communication flow of the source.

The source document does not contain formal bulleted or numbered lists, but rather sections introduced by bold headings (e.g., BAS, LUCA, PSAP). The conversion into headings and subsequent paragraphs correctly preserves the meaning and relationship of these items.

The email address 'Bob.Coats@osbm.nc.gov' from the source footer is correctly converted into a clickable mailto link in the output. No extraneous links were added, and no existing contact info was missed.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly converts the multi-column layout of the source PDF into a single-column, responsive format. The information from the left-hand sidebar boxes (Representation, Funding, Planning) is placed logically after the main introductory text. The icons and statistics from the bottom of PDF page 1 are moved to the top of the HTML, which provides a clear and appropriate introduction to the census data. The overall organization and flow of information are preserved and logical.

The timeline graphic on page 2 of the PDF, which displays key census activities from 2014 to 2021, has been successfully converted into a readable HTML table. The table correctly groups the activities under their respective date headers (2014-2016, 2017-2018, 2019-2021), ensuring that the temporal data remains accessible and understandable to a sighted user.

While decorative elements and some logos are present, a major meaningful infographic on the second page of the PDF is missing from the HTML output. This infographic is a diagram showing how five programs (LUCA, SDRP, PSAP, GSS, BAS) feed into a process funnel to result in 'Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', and 'Correct streets'. This graphic provides essential context for understanding the relationship between the programs described in the subsequent text blocks.
checks.b_evidence_source: Infographic on page 2 showing a funnel with inputs from LUCA, SDRP, PSAP, GSS, and BAS programs leading to 'Correct addresses', 'Correct boundaries', and 'Correct streets'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML content following the timeline table contains only text sections; the visual diagrammatic representation of program integration is entirely absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 2, right-hand side of the source PDF.

checks.a_analysis: There is no visual duplication of body content blocks such as paragraphs or tables. While headings from the source appear in the navigation 'Contents' list and again in the document body, this is a standard and expected accessibility feature for HTML conversion. The body content itself flows uniquely without repetition.
